title: Fresh Food & Real Help: How 211info and the Farm Direct Nutrition Program Support Older Adults - 211info
text: Food insecurity is a growing concern for many as prices continue to climb. But for older adults on fixed incomes, even basics like fresh, healthy food can feel out of reach. That’s where the Senior Farm Direct Nutrition Program (SFDNP) can help. SFDNP provides eligible seniors with vouchers to buy locally grown fruits, vegetables, and herbs from participating farmers’ markets and farm stands from June through November. The program not only helps older adults stretch their food budgets, but it also supports local farmers and strengthens our community food system. Research shows that programs like SFDNP have played a key role in creating and sustaining farmers’ markets in underserved communities, increasing access to fresh produce for everyone. Get Personalized Assistance 211info partners with the Oregon Health Authority to operate the Senior Farm Direct Helpline from April 1 to November 30 (program starts April 1, vouchers available for use June 1), Monday through Friday, 8 am-5 pm. Each year, thousands of older adults reach out with questions, and our SFDNP team provides one-on-one assistance to help them navigate the program. We walk callers through: If they’re eligible When and how they’ll get their vouchers Where they can use them What to do if they run into issues Sometimes, those conversations uncover additional needs, and our specialists can quickly tap into 211info’s database of 25,000+ local resources to connect callers with more help. “Fresh, nutritious food shouldn’t be out of reach just because of a tight budget, and programs like SFDNP make a difference—but only if people know how to access and use them. “That’s where we come in,” says Kelsi Sparrow, 211info’s SFDNP program lead. “In 2024 alone, we answered 2,860 calls from people seeking help navigating the program. And we strive to provide each an experience where when they hang up, they know they found someone who didn’t just check a box, but who actually listened, engaged, and was truly there to help.” In addition to SFDNP, 211info may also be able to connect older adults with: Aging and Disability Resource Connection (ADRC) Double-Up Food Bucks Home delivered meals Other nutrition and financial assistance programs We’re Here to Help At 211info, our mission is to ensure everyone can access the help they need. If you or someone you know could benefit from the Senior Farm Direct Nutrition Program, call our helpline at 866-299-3562. Our team is here to guide you through the process, answer your questions, and connect you with additional support if needed.
